prerequisites: A Mendeley account to sign in to the Mendeley Developer Portal at https://dev.mendeley.com/myapps.html and register an application.
A registered Mendeley API application, which provides the Application ID (client_id) and Secret (client_secret). The secret cannot be retrieved later, so record it at creation time.
A redirect URL configured on the registered application, used as the OAuth callback during the Authorization Code flow.

An OAuth 2.0 refresh token (client_refresh_token) obtained by completing the Authorization Code flow at least once; it is valid indefinitely and is used to mint new access tokens without re-authorizing.
Values for the required configuration fields: name_for_institution, query_for_catalog, and start_date.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code (refresh token)
authMethods.summary: Mendeley uses OAuth 2.0. The recommended Authorization Code flow registers an application to obtain a client_id (Application ID) and client_secret (Secret). Completing the flow returns an access token (valid one hour) and a refresh_token (valid indefinitely). This connector stores the client_id, client_secret, and client_refresh_token, then exchanges the refresh token at the token endpoint (POST grant_type=refresh_token) to obtain fresh access tokens for API requests.

setupSteps.title: Register a Mendeley API application
setupSteps.body: Sign in at https://dev.mendeley.com/myapps.html with your Mendeley account. Complete the registration form with an application name, a description, and a redirect URL (the OAuth callback). Click Generate Secret (or supply your own), submit, and immediately record the assigned Application ID and Secret, since the secret cannot be retrieved later.

setupSteps.title: Obtain client_id and client_secret
setupSteps.body: Use the Application ID from the registered app as client_id and the generated Secret as client_secret. These are the confidential credentials for the Authorization Code flow and are sent using HTTP Basic authentication when calling the token endpoint.

setupSteps.title: Complete the Authorization Code flow to get a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Direct a user to the authorization endpoint https://api.mendeley.com/oauth/authorize with client_id, redirect_uri, response_type=code, scope=all, and a state parameter. After the user signs in and authorizes, capture the returned authorization code at your redirect URL, then POST it to the token endpoint https://api.mendeley.com/oauth/token. The JSON response contains an access_token and a refresh_token; store the refresh_token as client_refresh_token.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials and configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter client_id, client_secret, and client_refresh_token as the secret credentials. Then set the required configuration fields: name_for_institution, query_for_catalog, and start_date. The connector refreshes access tokens automatically by POSTing grant_type=refresh_token with the stored refresh token to the token endpoint.

sources.title: Authorization Code Flow - Mendeley Developer Portal
